analysis_description: Round 3 tape IV smile width and vega-at-ATM from historical prices (days 0–2). TTE: CSV day d maps to DTE at open = 8−d per round3description example; intraday dte_eff = dte_open − (timestamp//100)/10000, T_years = dte_eff/365, r=0 (same as plot_iv_smile_round3). Implied vol from mid via Black–Scholes call + Brent; vega from closed form. Also summarized half-spread proxy from bid/ask mids in the script output file.
used_to_decide: IV smile width ~0.32–0.50 (median ~0.38 across sampled timestamps) suggests cross-strike mispricings vs a smooth surface can exceed typical half-spreads on some names; vega ATM declines as DTE shortens (8→6d). Guided initial residual hurdle ~1.35× half-spread and spline smoothing ~0.08 for a stable σ(log K) slice.

analysis_description: Small parameter grid on the same surface strategy (documented in results.json iteration 1): lower hurdle + smoother spline + smaller clip. Backtest total_profit 3486 vs v0 3883 on Round 3 days 0–2, worse fills — tighter hurdle increased turnover without improving edge.
used_to_decide: Keep v0 as parent for the next iteration; prefer v0’s higher hurdle and slightly less smoothed spline.
